_comment: M89 D1 SEEN labeled corpus (the A1 killing oracle), v1.3.0 3-result mechanical-filter design. 13 items hand-labeled. Input is a GUESSED CLAIM. The classifier is a MECHANICAL STRING-FACT FILTER returning internal | external | AMBIGUOUS: internal ONLY on a string-fact this-repo signal (concrete repo path/file/tool shape OR explicit this-repo anchor); external ONLY on an unambiguous vendor proper-noun co-occurring with an API/protocol term; AMBIGUOUS for everything else (semantic placement = the LLM judge's call, then uncertain->research). Items 1-7 (M87 findings) are repo-internal: 6 carry an explicit string fact (this-repo anchor or path/tool shape) -> internal; M87-F5 ('which contract FILE defines the disjointness rules') carries NO concrete string fact (generic 'contract file', no -contract.md shape) so it is AMBIGUOUS -> LLM judge -> grep resolves it internally (no silent miss). Items 8-13 (binvoice S2-M5): S2M5-F1 + S2M5-F4 carry an unambiguous vendor(PayPal)+api-term string fact with NO anchor -> external; S2M5-F5 (browser popup-blocker, NO vendor proper noun) is AMBIGUOUS -> LLM judge -> research; S2M5-F2 (invoice schema in this repo's Mongo) + S2M5-F3 (route handler in this repo) carry a this-repo anchor with NO strong external signal -> internal; S2M5-F6 (this-repo DB fields VS the PayPal API return) has BOTH a this-repo anchor AND a strong external signal (paypal+api) -> CONFLICT -> AMBIGUOUS (v1.3.2 class-closing rule: a generic anchor cannot override a strong external signal). The point of the oracle is unchanged: the classifier decides by string FACTS, never by guessing; and an anchor phrase NEVER routes an external-vendor claim internal (only a CONCRETE REPO PATH beats a strong external signal).

items.gap: Which domain in this repo owns the gsd-t-wave.workflow.js file?
What exit code does the gsd-t-verify-gate return when the Biome linter finds violations?
Does the current Workflow sandbox allow require() calls inside a .workflow.js file?
What is the shape of the envelope that gsd-t-competition-judge.cjs returns on a valid partition input?
Which contract file defines the disjointness rules for file-ownership across domains?
What test in this repo asserts that every workflow model: literal matches the tier policy?
How does the existing gsd-t-traceability-gate.cjs enumerate behavioral tasks vs non-behavioral tasks in tasks.md?
What is the exact endpoint path and required request body fields for the PayPal OAuth /v1/oauth2/token token-mint call?
What is the schema of the invoice object stored in the invoices collection in this repo's Mongo schema?
Which route handler in this repo handles the POST /invoices/send request?
What is the maximum total amount allowed on a single PayPal v2 invoice before the API returns an error?
Does the browser's built-in popup blocker block window.open() calls triggered from setTimeout handlers rather than direct user events?
What fields does the PayPal invoice draft stored in this repo's database contain vs what the API returns?
items._note: v1.3.2 class-closing rule: this claim has TWO subjects — this-repo DB fields (anchor 'this repo's') AND the external PayPal API return (vendor 'paypal' + api term). A generic anchor cannot override a strong external signal, so this is a genuine CONFLICT → ambiguous → LLM judge (which can split it: grep the DB fields, research the API return). Was 'internal' under the obsolete anchor-wins rule that allowed the silent-miss class; correctly ambiguous now.
